name: heimia salicifolia (sinicuichi)
mechanism: Available evidence is limited and does not establish a definitive human mechanism of action.
effects: Mild auditory and dream-related perceptual changes are described in traditional ethnobotanical reports.
contraindications: Avoid in pregnancy and with CNS depressants
avoid with alcohol or cns depressants
safetyNotes: but modern dosage should be conservative
CNS-active effects can occur; avoid combining with alcohol or sedative drugs.
high doses may cause mild sedation or nausea [9
description: Traditional Mexican herb used for altered states and memory recall
dosage: No standardized clinical dose is available; use only cautious, source-documented preparation practices.
duration: Duration appears preparation- and dose-dependent in traditional use reports; standardized human trial data are limited.
preparation: Traditional use includes infusion and fermented preparations; product concentration varies across preparations.
region: Native to Mexico and parts of Central and South America, with traditional use in regional ethnomedicine.
traditionalUse: Used in Aztec and Mexican rituals for memory recall and divination
summary: Heimia salicifolia has traditional ethnobotanical use for altered perception and calming effects, while modern controlled clinical evidence is limited.
